title: semecarpus ridleyi
intro: Semecarpus ridleyi, also known as the Malaysia stinking nut or stinking nut tree, is a plant species native to Malaysia and Indonesia, characterized by its unique, foul-smelling seed pods. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The seeds of Semecarpus ridleyi have been used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to their analgesic, anti-pyretic, and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, the seeds are often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, and can also be crushed and applied topically to relieve pain and inflammation.
